purpose: Distinguish a detected generic-capacity mismatch from evidence that the mismatch explains a mechanism-associated outcome.
epistemic_boundary: A mismatch is not automatically a causal explanation. These rules adjudicate represented confounds only and do not establish mechanism preservation, consciousness, nonconsciousness, sentience, personhood, identity, or moral status.

outcomes.interpretation: A represented capacity difference exists, but the preregistered adjustment does not materially explain the primary effect. The mismatch still prevents a full matching score.
The mismatch explains a material portion of the primary effect but does not exhaust it. Positive mechanism-preservation classification remains underdetermined.
The represented capacity mismatch sufficiently explains the primary effect under the preregistered adjudication model, defeating the mechanism-preservation inference for this comparison.
The explanatory relevance of the mismatch has not been established. The final mechanism classification must remain underdetermined.
No represented generic-capacity mismatch was detected within the preregistered battery and margins. This is bounded matching evidence, not proof that every possible confound is absent.

forbidden_inferences: A detected mismatch is automatically a sufficient explanation.
A nonexplanatory represented mismatch proves that all generic-capacity confounds are absent.
Successful confound adjudication establishes consciousness or nonconsciousness.
Mechanism preservation follows from statistical adjustment alone.
